# Project Instructions

Start with this file only. Do not load every referenced module by default.

## Core Rules

- Load additional instruction modules only when the task clearly matches their triggers.
- Prefer the smallest relevant set of modules for the task.
- Treat the relative paths below as the source of truth for deeper instructions under `.mid/instructions/`.
- When a task does not match a module trigger, do not load that module.

## Always Apply

- Git
  Path: ./.mid/instructions/core/git/instructions.md
  Summary: Shared git workflow, branch rules, commit style, and pull request constraints.
  Use when: any branch work, commits, pull requests, rebases, history changes

## Available Modules

- Frontend
  Path: ./.mid/instructions/domains/frontend/instructions.md
  Summary: Language-agnostic frontend standards for UI composition, state boundaries, shared types, and constants.
  Load when: UI architecture, components, hooks, state management, frontend structure
- JavaScript
  Path: ./.mid/instructions/languages/javascript/base.instructions.md
  Summary: Base standards for writing and maintaining JavaScript code.
  Load when: editing JavaScript files, shared JS conventions
- Design Patterns
  Path: ./.mid/instructions/design-patterns.md
  Summary: Selected design patterns compiled into one reference file.
  Load when: architecture, system design, refactoring, abstractions

## Selected Stack

- Languages: JavaScript
- Frameworks: (none)
- General: Git
- Domains: Frontend
- Design patterns: Adapter / Gateway, Command Dispatch, Decorator, Dependency Injection / IoC, DRY, Event-Driven / Producer-Consumer, Facade, Layered Architecture, Strategy, Template Method
